title: Waveguide- and cavity-backed antenna arrays with distributed signal amplifiers for transmission of a high-power beam
abstract: An antenna that has phase-shifting ability without expensive and large phase shifters includes a cavity, signal couplers, and antenna elements. The cavity is configured to reinforce a reference signal, and each of the signal couplers is configured to generate a respective intermediate signal in response to the reference signal at a respective location of the cavity. And each of the antenna element is configured to radiate a respective element signal in response to an intermediate signal from a respective one of the signal couplers.
claims:
Claims 1 . An antenna, comprising: a transmission structure configured to carry a reference signal; couplers each configured to generate a respective intermediate signal in response to the reference signal at a respective location along the transmission structure; amplifiers each configured to amplify, selectively, an intermediate signal from a respective one of the couplers; and antenna elements each configured to radiate a respective elemental signal in response to an amplified intermediate signal from a respective one of the amplifiers. 2. The antenna of claim 1 wherein the transmission structure includes a waveguide. 3. The antenna of claim 1 or 2 wherein: the transmission structure includes a conductive wall; and each of the couplers includes a respective iris formed through the conductive wall and configured to generate a respective intermediate signal in response to the reference signal. 4. The antenna of any of claims 1 -3 wherein each amplifier includes a respective transistor having a first conduction node coupled to a respective one of the couplers and having a second conduction node coupled to a respective one of the antenna elements. 5. The antenna of claim 1 wherein: the transmission structure includes a waveguide having a wall; and each of the couplers includes a respective iris disposed in the wall and configured to generate a respective iris signal in response to the reference signal, and a respective radiator extending approximately parallel to the iris and configured to radiate the respective intermediate signal in response to the respective iris signal. 6. The antenna of claim 1 wherein: the reference signal has a first frequency; the transmission structure includes a waveguide having a wall; and each of the couplers includes a respective iris disposed in the wall and configured to generate, in response to the reference signal, a respective iris signal having the first frequency, a respective signal mixer configured to receive a respective mixing signal having a second frequency; and a respective coupling region disposed between the respective iris and the respective signal mixer, and configured to form, with the respective signal mixer, a circuit configured to resonate at the first frequency; and the respective signal mixer is configured to generate the respective intermediate signal having a frequency approximately equal to a sum or to a difference of the first frequency and the second frequency. 7. A method, comprising: generating intermediate signals in response to a reference signal at respective tap locations along a transmission structure through which the reference signal is propagating; amplifying at least one of the intermediate signals; generating a respective elemental signal in response to each of the at least one of the amplified intermediate signals; and forming a beam pattern by radiating each elemental signal from a respective radiating location. 8. The method of claim 7 wherein amplifying at least one of the intermediate signals includes amplifying each of the intermediate signals that corresponds to an asserted control signal with a gain that is related to a value of the asserted control signal. 9. The method of claim 7 or 8 wherein generating the intermediate signals includes: generating a respective iris signal with a respective iris formed in a wall of the transmission structure at a respective one of the tap locations; and generating each intermediate signal in response to a respective iris signal. 10. The method of any of claims 7-9, further comprising: wherein forming the beam pattern includes forming a beam having a first orientation; and steering the beam from the first orientation to the second orientation. 11. The method of any of claims 7-9, further comprising: wherein forming the beam pattern includes forming a beam; determining a position of an object; and steering the beam to intersect the object. 12. The method of any of claims 7-9, further comprising: wherein forming the beam pattern includes forming a beam; tracking a position of a moving object; and steering the beam in response to the tracked position to power the object. 13. An antenna, comprising: a cavity configured to reinforce a reference signal; couplers configured to generate intermediate signals in response to the reference signal at respective locations within the cavity; and antenna elements each configured to radiate a respective elemental signal in response to an intermediate signal from a respective one of the couplers. 14. The antenna of claim 13, further comprising tuning structure disposed within the cavity and configured to shift a phase of the reference signal at one or more of the respective locations within the cavity in response to a tuning signal. 15. The antenna of claim 13, further comprising tuning structure disposed within the cavity and configured to shift an amplitude of the reference signal at 

 one or more of the respective locations within the cavity in response to a tuning signal. 16. The antenna of any of claims 13-15 wherein: the cavity includes a conductive wall; and each of the couplers includes a respective iris formed through the conductive wall adjacent to a respective one of the locations and configured to generate a respective one of the intermediate signals in response to the reference signal. 17. The antenna of any of claims 13-16, further comprising: amplifiers each configured to amplify a respective one of the intermediate signals; and wherein each one of the antenna elements is configured to radiate a respective elemental signal in response to an amplified intermediate signal from a respective one of the amplifiers. 18. The antenna of claim 1 wherein: the cavity includes a conductive wall; and each of the couplers includes a respective iris disposed in the wall adjacent to a respective one of the locations, and a respective conductor extending at least partially into the iris and configured to generate a respective intermediate signal in response to the reference signal. 19. A method, comprising: reinforcing a reference signal in a cavity; generating intermediate signals in response to the reference signal at respective locations within the cavity; and radiating a respective elemental signal in response to a respective one of the intermediate signals. 20. The method of claim 19, further comprising shifting a phase of the reference signal at one or more of the respective locations within the cavity in response to a tuning signal. 21. The method of claim 19 or 20, further comprising shifting an amplitude of the reference signal at one or more of the respective locations within the cavity in response to a tuning signal. 22. The method of any of claims 19-21 , further comprising: amplifying one or more of the intermediate signals; and wherein radiating a respective elemental signal includes radiating each of one or more of the elemental signals in response to a respective amplified one of the one or more intermediate signals. 23. The method of any of claims 19-22, further comprising forming a beam pattern by radiating each elemental signal from a respective radiating location. 24. The method of claim 23, further comprising: wherein forming the beam pattern includes forming a beam; and powering an object with the beam. 25. The method of claim 23, further comprising: wherein forming the beam pattern includes forming a beam; determining a position of an object; and steering the beam in response to the determined position to power the object. 26. The method of claim 23, further comprising: wherein forming the beam pattern includes forming a beam; tracking a position of a moving object; and steering the beam in response to the tracked position to power the object.
